presentation.chiefComplaint: Crushing chest pain radiating to the left arm for 40 minutes

history.hpi: Sudden substernal pressure that began while mowing the lawn, radiating to the left arm and jaw, associated with diaphoresis, nausea, and shortness of breath. Pain rated 9/10, not relieved by rest.
history.socialHistory: One pack per day smoker for 30 years.
redFlags: Crushing chest pain radiating to arm/jaw with diaphoresis
High-risk cardiac presentation requiring immediate ECG

expert.esiRationale: High-risk chest pain concerning for acute coronary syndrome. Hemodynamically stable and not requiring an immediate life-saving intervention at the door (so not ESI 1), but a time-critical, high-risk presentation that cannot wait. ESI 2.

provenance.sourceRef: ED Triage Trainer hand-authored seed case
